prerequisites: An Exchange Rates API account. Sign up for a free account at exchangeratesapi.io to obtain credentials.
An API access key (access_key) issued to your account. This key authenticates every request.
A start_date in YYYY-MM-DD format, used as the earliest date for which historical rates are retrieved.
If you intend to set a non-default base currency, a subscription plan that supports changing the base currency. The default base is EUR and the free plan is limited to EUR.

authMethods.name: API access key
authMethods.summary: Exchange Rates API authenticates requests with a single API access key passed as the access_key URL parameter. Obtain the key from your exchangeratesapi.io account and provide it in the connector's access_key field.

setupSteps.title: Create an Exchange Rates API account
setupSteps.body: Go to exchangeratesapi.io and sign up. A free account is sufficient to obtain an API access key, though some features such as changing the base currency require a paid subscription plan.

setupSteps.title: Copy your API access key
setupSteps.body: After signing up, retrieve your unique API access key from your account dashboard. The API identifies you by this key, which is passed into every request as the access_key URL parameter (for example, https://api.exchangeratesapi.io/v1/latest?access_key=API_KEY).

setupSteps.title: Provide the access key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your API access key in the required access_key field. This is the only credential the connector needs to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date to sync, in YYYY-MM-DD format. The API serves historical rates by appending a date in this format to the base URL (for example, https://api.exchangeratesapi.io/v1/2013-12-24?access_key=API_KEY).

setupSteps.title: Optionally set base currency and weekend handling
setupSteps.body: Optionally set the base field to a three-letter currency code to change the base currency that output rates are relative to. The default base is EUR, and changing it requires a subscription plan that supports it (the free plan only allows EUR). Optionally enable ignore_weekends to skip weekend dates when syncing.

sources.title: Exchange Rates API documentation (authentication and access_key)
Exchange Rates API pricing (plan tiers and base currency support)
